title: AI service — Gemini summarization and retrieval
description: Create src/services/ai.ts wrapping the Google Generative AI SDK (@google/generative-ai). Use gemini-2.5-flash model. Implement two functions: summarizeConversation(topic, messages) — takes a topic string and array of formatted messages, returns a concise summary capturing names, numbers, decisions, action items. answerFromNotes(query, notes) — takes a user query and matching notes, returns a natural language answer citing sources. Use low temperature (~0.3) for factual responses. System prompt establishes the bot as a note-taking assistant.

title: Bot entry point — wire everything together
description: Create src/index.ts as the main bot entry point. Set up Discord.js Client with intents: Guilds, GuildMessages, MessageContent. On 'ready' event: log bot username and server count, initialize the database schema. On 'interactionCreate': route slash commands to the appropriate handler (save, recall, notes). Load environment variables via dotenv. Handle graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM. Add npm scripts: 'dev' (tsx watch src/index.ts), 'start' (node dist/index.js), 'build' (tsc).

title: Document storage — database schema and CRUD
description: Add a 'documents' table to the Turso schema in db.ts: id, server_id, title, file_type (pdf/docx/txt/image), original_filename, extracted_text, uploaded_by_id, uploaded_by_name, created_at. Implement CRUD functions: saveDocument(), searchDocuments(serverId, query) using LIKE on title+extracted_text, listDocuments(serverId, limit), deleteDocument(id, serverId). Large documents may need to be chunked — store chunks as separate rows with a parent_document_id.

title: Project scaffolding
description: Initialize the Node.js TypeScript project with all configuration files. Create package.json with discord.js, @google/generative-ai, @libsql/client, and dotenv dependencies. Set up tsconfig.json for ES modules. Create the src/ directory structure (commands/, services/). Add dev scripts for tsx watch mode and build scripts for tsc compilation.

title: /upload command implementation
description: Create src/commands/upload.ts implementing the /upload slash command. Flow: 1) User attaches a file and provides a 'title' option, 2) Bot defers reply, 3) Download the attachment from Discord's CDN, 4) Detect file type from extension/mime type, 5) Extract text using the extractor service, 6) Store extracted text + metadata in Turso documents table, 7) Reply with embed showing: title, file type, character count of extracted text, preview of first ~200 chars, who uploaded it. Validate: file size under 25MB (Discord limit), supported file types only.

title: /notes command implementation
description: Create src/commands/notes.ts implementing the /notes slash command. Flow: 1) Receive interaction, 2) Query listNotes(serverId) from Turso, 3) If no notes, reply saying no notes saved yet, 4) Format as a Discord embed with: blue color, 'Saved Notes' title, numbered list showing topic, date, and first ~100 chars of summary as preview for each note. Show total count in footer. Limit to 10 notes per page initially (pagination can come later).

title: Slash command registration script
description: Create src/deploy-commands.ts as a standalone script to register slash commands with Discord's API using discord.js REST. Register three commands: /save (required 'topic' string option — what to save), /recall (required 'query' string option — what to search for), /notes (no options — lists all saved notes). Uses DISCORD_TOKEN and DISCORD_CLIENT_ID from env. Run once with 'npm run deploy-commands' to register globally.

title: End-to-end testing and verification
description: Test the bot end-to-end in a real Discord server. Verify: 1) Bot starts and logs 'online' message, 2) /save command reads recent messages, summarizes correctly, stores in Turso, shows confirmation embed, 3) /recall command finds saved notes, returns AI-powered answer with source citations, 4) /notes command lists all saved notes with previews, 5) Notes are scoped per server (multi-server isolation), 6) Error handling works (empty channels, no matching notes, API failures). Fix any issues found.

title: /save command implementation
description: Create src/commands/save.ts implementing the /save slash command. Flow: 1) Receive interaction with 'topic' option, 2) Defer reply (ephemeral thinking message), 3) Fetch last 50 messages from channel using message service, 4) Send messages + topic to Gemini summarization, 5) Store summary + raw messages in Turso via db service, 6) Reply with a Discord embed showing: green color, 'Note Saved' title, topic name, the AI summary, who saved it, timestamp. Handle errors gracefully with user-facing error message.

title: Discord message fetching utility
description: Create src/services/messages.ts with a function fetchRecentMessages(channel, limit=50) that uses channel.messages.fetch() to get recent messages. Filter out bot messages. Format each message as '@Username (3:42 PM): message content' with timestamps for AI context. Return as a structured array and also as a formatted string for the AI prompt.

title: /delete command — remove notes and documents
description: Create src/commands/delete.ts implementing the /delete slash command. Takes an 'id' option (integer) and a 'type' option (choice: note or document). Deletes the specified item from the appropriate table, but only if it belongs to the current server (security check). Confirms deletion with an embed. Only the person who created the note/document or a server admin should be able to delete it.

title: /docs command — list uploaded documents
description: Create src/commands/docs.ts implementing the /docs slash command. Lists all uploaded documents for the current server. Show: title, file type icon/label, original filename, upload date, who uploaded it, and extracted text character count. Format as a Discord embed, similar to /notes. Limit to 10 per page.

title: Enhanced /recall — search notes AND documents
description: Modify the existing /recall command to search both the notes table AND the documents table. Combine results from both sources and send them to Gemini as context. The AI response should cite whether the answer came from a conversation note or an uploaded document, including the document title or note topic and date. If results come from both sources, present a unified answer that synthesizes information from both.

title: V2: Document Storage and Retrieval
description: Add the ability to upload and store documents (PDFs, Word docs, images, text files) to the bot. The bot extracts text content, stores it alongside conversation notes, and includes document content when answering /recall queries. This turns the bot from a conversation memory tool into a full team knowledge base.
notes: Design plan: .claude/plans/zippy-spinning-plum.md — V2 section covers /upload command, text extraction (PDF/Word/image OCR), documents table schema, enhanced /recall searching notes+docs, and /docs listing command.

title: Database service — Turso connection and schema
description: Create src/services/db.ts with Turso (libsql) client connection. Implement schema initialization (CREATE TABLE notes with id, server_id, channel_id, topic, summary, raw_messages JSON, created_by_id, created_by_name, created_at). Implement CRUD functions: saveNote(), searchNotes(serverId, query) using LIKE on topic+summary, listNotes(serverId, limit), deleteNote(id, serverId). Connection uses TURSO_DATABASE_URL and TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN from env.

title: /recall command implementation
description: Create src/commands/recall.ts implementing the /recall slash command. Flow: 1) Receive interaction with 'query' option, 2) Defer reply, 3) Search notes in Turso using db.searchNotes(serverId, query), 4) If no matches found, reply saying no relevant notes were found, 5) If matches found, send matching notes + query to Gemini answerFromNotes(), 6) Reply with a Discord embed showing: blue color, 'Memory Recall' title, the AI answer, source note references with dates. Handle errors gracefully.

title: V1: Conversation Memory
description: Core conversation memory features: save, recall, and list notes from Discord chat using AI summarization (Gemini) and cloud storage (Turso). The bot should let small teams save important discussions and retrieve them later with natural language queries.
notes: Design plan: .claude/plans/zippy-spinning-plum.md — contains full architecture, database schema, command flows, tech stack decisions, build order, and verification steps for both V1 and V2.

title: Text extraction service — PDF, Word, images
description: Create src/services/extractor.ts with functions to extract text from different file types. Use pdf-parse for PDFs, mammoth for Word docs (.docx), direct read for .txt/.md files. For images, use Gemini's vision capability to OCR/describe the image content. Each function takes a Buffer and returns extracted text as a string. Handle extraction failures gracefully (corrupted files, password-protected PDFs, etc.).

title: Shared TypeScript types
description: Create src/types.ts with shared interfaces: Note (id, server_id, channel_id, topic, summary, raw_messages, created_by_id, created_by_name, created_at), FormattedMessage (author, content, timestamp), SaveNoteParams (server_id, channel_id, topic, summary, raw_messages, created_by_id, created_by_name). These types are used across commands and services.
